2603.22386.title: From Static Templates to Dynamic Runtime Graphs: A Survey of Workflow Optimization for LLM Agents
2603.22386.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM)-based systems are becoming increasingly popular for solving tasks by constructing executable workflows that interleave LLM calls, information retrieval, tool use, code execution, memory updates, and verification. This survey reviews recent methods for designing and optimizing such workflows, which we treat as agentic computation graphs (ACGs). We organize the literature based on when workflow structure is determined, where structure refers to which components or agents are present, how they depend on each other, and how information flows between them. This lens distinguishes static methods, which fix a reusable workflow scaffold before deployment, from dynamic methods, which select, generate, or revise the workflow for a particular run before or during execution. We further organize prior work along three dimensions: when structure is determined, what part of the workflow is optimized, and which evaluation signals guide optimization (e.g., task metrics, verifier signals, preferences, or trace-derived feedback). We also distinguish reusable workflow templates, run-specific realized graphs, and execution traces, separating reusable design choices from the structures actually deployed in a given run and from realized runtime behavior. Finally, we outline a structure-aware evaluation perspective that complements downstream task metrics with graph-level properties, execution cost, robustness, and structural variation across inputs. Our goal is to provide a clear vocabulary, a unified framework for positioning new methods, a more comparable view of existing body of literature, and a more reproducible evaluation standard for future work in workflow optimizations for LLM agents.
2603.22386.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）基礎的系統在解決任務方面越來越受歡迎，通過構建可執行的工作流程，交錯進行LLM調用、信息檢索、工具使用、代碼執行、內存更新和驗證。本調查回顧了設計和優化這些工作流程的最新方法，我們將其視為具代理計算圖（ACGs）。我們根據工作流程結構確定的時間來組織文獻，其中結構指的是哪些組件或代理存在，它們之間的依賴關係，以及信息如何在它們之間流動。這種視角區分了靜態方法，這些方法在部署之前固定一個可重用的工作流程框架，與動態方法，這些方法在執行之前或期間為特定運行選擇、生成或修訂工作流程。我們進一步沿著三個維度組織先前的工作：結構確定的時間、工作流程的哪一部分被優化，以及哪些評估信號指導優化（例如，任務指標、驗證信號、偏好或基於追踪的反饋）。我們還區分了可重用的工作流程模板、特定運行的實現圖和執行追踪，將可重用的設計選擇與在特定運行中實際部署的結構和實現的運行時行為分開。最後，我們概述了一種結構感知的評估視角，該視角通過圖級屬性、執行成本、穩健性和輸入之間的結構變化來補充下游任務指標。我們的目標是提供一個清晰的詞彙、一個統一的框架來定位新方法、一個更可比較的現有文獻視圖，以及一個更可重複的評估標準，以促進未來在LLM代理的工作流程優化方面的研究。
2603.22386.author: Ling Yue et.al.
2603.22386.authors: Ling Yue, Kushal Raj Bhandari, Ching-Yun Ko, Dhaval Patel, Shuxin Lin, Nianjun Zhou, Jianxi Gao, Pin-Yu Chen, Shaowu Pan
